formulas.name: 1. Species-Area Relationship
formulas.variables: S = number of species, A = area, Z = constant, b = regression coefficient
formulas.notes: Used to describe the relationship between the area of a habitat and the number of species found within it

formulas.name: 2. Shannon-Wiener Diversity Index
formulas.variables: H = diversity index, pi = proportion of each species
formulas.notes: Used to calculate the diversity of a community based on the proportion of each species

formulas.name: 3. Simpson Diversity Index
formulas.variables: D = diversity index, ni = number of individuals of each species, N = total number of individuals
formulas.notes: Used to calculate the diversity of a community based on the number of individuals of each species

formulas.name: 4. Population Growth Rate
formulas.variables: r = intrinsic growth rate, dN = change in population size, dt = change in time, N = population size
formulas.notes: Used to describe the rate of change of a population over time

formulas.name: 5. Logistic Growth Equation
formulas.variables: dN = change in population size, dt = change in time, r = intrinsic growth rate, N = population size, K = carrying capacity
formulas.notes: Used to describe the growth of a population in an environment with limited resources

formulas.name: 6. Carrying Capacity
formulas.variables: K = carrying capacity, food = available food, water = available water, shelter = available shelter
formulas.notes: Used to describe the maximum population size that an environment can sustain indefinitely

formulas.name: 7. Natality
formulas.variables: Natality = birth rate, number of births = number of new individuals added to the population, population size = total number of individuals
formulas.units: Per 100 individuals per unit time (e.g., per year)
formulas.notes: Used to describe the rate at which new individuals are added to a population

formulas.name: 8. Mortality
formulas.variables: Mortality = death rate, number of deaths = number of individuals that die, population size = total number of individuals
formulas.units: Per 100 individuals per unit time (e.g., per year)
formulas.notes: Used to describe the rate at which individuals die in a population

formulas.name: 9. Immigration
formulas.variables: Immigration = rate of new individuals entering the population, number of immigrants = number of new individuals entering the population, population size = total number of individuals
formulas.units: Per 100 individuals per unit time (e.g., per year)
formulas.notes: Used to describe the rate at which new individuals enter a population

formulas.name: 10. Emigration
formulas.variables: Emigration = rate of individuals leaving the population, number of emigrants = number of individuals leaving the population, population size = total number of individuals
formulas.units: Per 100 individuals per unit time (e.g., per year)
formulas.notes: Used to describe the rate at which individuals leave a population
